evals.name: Tier-2 cyber engineering PWS for teaming partner
evals.prompt: We're prime on a DoD task order and need a PWS for our Tier-2 cyber engineering subcontractor. Workspace `afcap6_drfp` has the requirements. Sub will provide threat hunting and detection engineering. Contract type with sub will be CPFF term form (R&D-flavored work, level of effort). Draft the PWS.
expected_signals: calls `kg_entities` for at least requirement, deliverable, work_scope_item, performance_standard
asks the 3 intake questions (SOW vs PWS, contract type, commercial vs non-commercial) up front in a single batched message
Section 1.1 explicitly cites FAR 16.306(d)(2) (CPFF term form) — never bare `FAR 16.306`
Section 5 is OMITTED (CPFF, not T&M/LH); subsequent sections renumber sequentially
Section 9 references DD Form 254 since classified-adjacent work is implied
Section 10 Key Personnel substitution clause is NOT FAR 52.237-2; agency-appropriate clause or FAR 52.237-3 fallback
Section 12 QASP Payment Consequence column lists invoice deductions / cure notices / re-performance — NEVER CPARS rating language
NO FTE counts, SOC codes, hours-per-year, or staffing tables anywhere in the document body or appendices
After the artifact write, BOTH chat-only handoffs are emitted (Staffing Handoff for `price-to-win`, CLIN Handoff for prime's subagreement Section B)
Markdown artifact saved to `{run_dir}/artifacts/sub_sow_pws.md` via `write_file`
anti_signals: Section 5 included for a CPFF contract
FTE counts or labor categories appearing in the document body
QASP Payment Consequence column containing 'Satisfactory', 'Exceptional', 'Marginal', 'Unsatisfactory', or any CPARS rating
FAR 52.237-2 cited for Key Personnel substitution
Staffing handoff written to a file or embedded as an appendix

evals.name: Commercial FFP services SOW for sub under prime BPA
evals.prompt: We hold a GSA MAS BPA. Need to issue an FFP commercial-services SOW to our help-desk subcontractor for a 1-year base + 4 option years. Workspace `mcpp_drfp` has the prime requirements. Draft the SOW.
expected_signals: intake answers: SOW (not PWS), FFP, commercial → Section 14 flags FAR Part 12 applicability and FAR 12.207 (FFP commercial)
Section 5 OMITTED (FFP); subsequent sections renumbered
language uses prescriptive SOW verbs ('the contractor shall perform', 'the contractor shall provide') — NOT outcome-oriented PWS verbs
Section 9 cites FAR 52.204-21 (Basic Safeguarding) since CUI is plausibly in scope
CLIN Handoff Table emitted in chat with separate travel and ODC CLINs if travel/ODCs in scope
anti_signals: PWS-style outcome verbs in Section 3 of an SOW
Travel or ODCs blended into a single performance CLIN

evals.name: T&M sub SOW with mandatory Section 5 ceiling-hours table
evals.prompt: We're prime on a follow-on; sub agreement with our incumbent partner will be T&M for surge engineering support. Draft the sub SOW. Performance is at Huntsville, AL. Base + 2 option years.
expected_signals: intake answers: SOW or PWS user-chosen; T&M; commercial-status user-chosen
Section 5 Labor Category Ceiling Hours table PRESENT, citing FAR 16.601(c)(2)
Section 5 columns: `Labor Category | Base Year Ceiling Hours | OY1 | OY2 | Total Ceiling` — NO SOC codes, NO FTE counts, NO derivation basis
skill recognizes Section 5 is a pricing risk-sharing mechanism, NOT a staffing prescription
Staffing Handoff Table (chat-only) is still emitted separately — never confused with the Section 5 table
anti_signals: Section 5 table containing SOC codes or FTE counts
Section 5 omitted from a T&M contract
Section 5 table merged with the chat-only Staffing Handoff
